programs.name: MassHealth Medicare Savings Program
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare Part B premiums and cost-sharing for low-income seniors (formerly Senior Buy-In). Includes QMB (full premiums/cost-sharing), SLMB, and QI levels.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries 65+ with low income/assets meeting federal MSP thresholds.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Part B premium ~$174.70/month paid; QMB covers deductibles/copays up to $8,130/year income limit.

programs.name: Frail Elder Waiver (FEW)
programs.what_it_does: Provides home and community-based services waiver for nursing home qualified seniors to avoid institutionalization. Includes personal care, homemaker services, home modifications, meal delivery, and emergency response systems.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ who qualify for nursing home level care, including those with Alzheimer's/dementia; income ≤$2,829/month, assets ≤$2,000.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Variety of in-home supports valued at thousands monthly equivalent to institutional care costs.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits into comprehensive care including long-term services, dental, and eye care. Delivered through adult day health centers with home-based support.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Dual-eligible seniors 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible, living in PACE service area.[1]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitation covering full Medicare/Medicaid LTC benefits plus extras.

programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly electronic benefits for food purchases to low-income seniors. Administered statewide by DTA with senior-specific deductions.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income ≤130-200% FPL depending on household; higher asset limits for elderly.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+/month per senior household for groceries.

programs.name: Fuel Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay heating costs during winter for eligible low-income households. Provides one-time grants applied directly to fuel bills.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ in households ≤60% state median income; automatic eligibility for SSI recipients.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Grants $400-$1,300 per household based on size/income.

programs.name: Massachusetts Weatherization Program
programs.what_it_does: Insulates homes, seals leaks, and upgrades heating systems to reduce energy costs. Free services for qualified low-income households.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ meeting LIHEAP-like income guidelines.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements saving $300+/year on energy bills.

programs.name: SHINE (Serving Health Information Needs of Elders)
programs.what_it_does: Free Medicare counseling and enrollment assistance provided by trained volunteers. Helps compare plans and resolve issues.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Personalized counseling saving thousands in premiums/coverage gaps.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious hot meals to homebound seniors daily. Provides over 70,000 meals/day statewide, free for most.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals; income-based but often free.[3]

programs.name: Caregiver Respite Program
programs.what_it_does: Funds temporary relief care for family caregivers of elders. Offered through Area Agencies on Aging with vouchers or services.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ needing assistance with daily activities.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $25/hour respite vouchers; annual cap ~$1,600.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service jobs with training for unemployed low-income seniors. Leads to unsubsidized employment.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed 55+ with income ≤125% FPL; priority for hardest-to-serve.[3]

programs.name: Massachusetts Senior Legal Helpline
programs.what_it_does: Free legal advice and representation for civil issues affecting seniors. Covers housing, benefits, consumer issues via statewide hotline.[3]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living resolving complaints. Protects rights and investigates abuse/neglect.[3]
programs.who_its_for: All residents of long-term care facilities in Massachusetts.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services preventing unnecessary institutionalization.

programs.name: Prescription Advantage
programs.what_it_does: Provides prescription drug coverage to help pay for medications not fully covered by Medicare. It offers financial assistance for co-pays and premiums for eligible seniors and disabled individuals.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts residents age 65 and older, or younger individuals with disabilities who meet income and employment guidelines.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical assistance program specific to Massachusetts, supplementing federal Medicare Part D for those who don't qualify for federal low-income subsidies.
programs.benefit_value: Reduces out-of-pocket prescription costs; exact amount varies by income and drug costs, with premiums starting around $30/month for some plans.

programs.name: Home Care Program (HCP)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers in-home services like personal care, homemaking, meal preparation, and transportation to support aging in place. Administered through Aging Services Access Points (ASAPs) with personalized care plans developed by social workers.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts seniors, people with disabilities, and those with early-stage dementia who need help with activities of daily living (ADLs) and meet residency and functional needs criteria.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program unique to Massachusetts, contracted through 27 non-profit ASAPs providing comprehensive case management and services beyond standard federal HCBS waivers.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; services on sliding scale co-pays, potentially thousands in annual in-home support value.

programs.name: Enhanced Community Options Program (ECOP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides intensive in-home care and non-medical assistance for those needing a higher level of support than standard home care. Includes care management, personal care, and coordination to prevent nursing home placement.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts elders requiring greater care than HCP, assessed via ADLs, often at risk of institutionalization.
programs.whats_unique: Massachusetts-specific program for higher-needs seniors, distinct from federal options, with sliding scale fees through Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive services on sliding scale; high value in avoiding nursing home costs (average $100K+/year).

programs.name: Home Modification and Loan Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers low-interest loans for home adaptations like ramps, stair lifts, and bathroom modifications to promote independent living. Helps seniors age in place by making homes accessible.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts homeowners, primarily seniors and disabled individuals needing accessibility modifications.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific 0%-3% interest loans up to $30,000 administered by the Massachusetts Rehabilitation Commission, not a standard federal offering.
programs.benefit_value: Loans up to $30,000 at 0%-3% interest, saving thousands in interest compared to commercial loans.

programs.name: Senior Care Options (SCO)
programs.what_it_does: Integrates Medicare and MassHealth benefits into a single managed care plan with personalized care teams for health and social services. Coordinates doctor visits, long-term care, and home-based support.
programs.who_its_for: Dual-eligible Massachusetts seniors 65+ enrolled in both Medicare and MassHealth.
programs.whats_unique: Massachusetts-specific integrated care model blending state Medicaid (MassHealth) with federal Medicare, featuring dedicated care teams.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive coverage with no additional premiums; value in coordinated care reducing out-of-pocket expenses.
